Why I Built This

I Watched Installers
Burn Their Ad Budget
For 3 Years.

I have been running Meta ads for over 3 years. When I started working in the solar industry, I kept seeing the same mistake installers running ads that sent people directly to their WhatsApp. Every click cost money. A curious student, a renter, someone just comparing prices all clicking, all costing you.

So I built a system that sits between the Facebook ad and your WhatsApp. Eight gates. Wrong answer on any one of them they never reach you and you never pay. You only pay when someone who is genuinely ready to buy arrives in your inbox.

3+ Years running
Facebook Ads
8 Gates built to
filter time-wasters
₦0 Charged for leads
that don't qualify

Your Ad Spend Is Working.
Your Filter Isn't.

Not A Decision Maker

You are not getting bad results because your ads are wrong. You are getting bad results because nobody is qualifying the people who see them. Every one of those messages represents wasted ad spend, wasted follow-up time, and a wasted site visit. We qualify all leads before a single number reaches your phone.

What ₦5,000 Actually Buys You

8 Checks Run on Every Lead
Before You Pay a Single Naira.

You do not pay until a homeowner passes all 8. That means ₦5,000 is not the cost of a lead it is the cost of a lead that has already been screened, verified, and confirmed ready. Here is every check we run before you are charged.

Most agencies send you whoever clicked the ad. We send you whoever survived the filter. That is why our partners close 38% of their leads when the industry average is under 10%.

The 8 gates are not a questionnaire. They are a conditional qualification system built into the Facebook form itself — wrong answers disqualify automatically, before the lead is ever created.

The result: every homeowner you call has already confirmed they own the property, have the budget, have a timeline, have a suitable home, and are expecting your call.

How You Get More
From the Same Facebook Budget.

You are already spending on Facebook every week. That does not change. What changes is what comes out of it, because now every naira of that spend runs through our 8-gate qualification system before a single lead reaches your phone.

01
Pay Facebook directly
You send your ad budget to Facebook. We run it through our managed Facebook ad account which protects your investment because we carry all the campaign infrastructure, creative, and pixel data. Minimum ₦50,000 per week, no upper limit.
managed by Tubalcain

We Build & Launch
We write the ad creative, build the qualification system, and launch your campaign within 48 hours. You approve the creative before anything goes live.

8 Gates Filter Every Lead
Every respondent passes through our 8-question qualification system. Budget, ownership, timeline, home safety, location — all confirmed before delivery.
Zero time-wasters

Pay Per Lead Delivered
When a qualified homeowner lands in your WhatsApp you pay ₦5,000 (leads only) or ₦7,000 (leads + follow-up). Zero leads delivered means zero charge from Tubalcain — your ad spend went to Facebook, the same as if you ran the ads yourself.

Why the Complete System at ₦7,000 Per Lead Pays for Itself

The Problem
No One Talks About.

Getting pre-qualified homeowners to your WhatsApp is step one. What happens next determines whether they become ₦2M jobs or silent numbers. Most installers lose deals not because of bad leads but because of what happens after the lead arrives.

Scenario 1
"A qualified homeowner filled the form on Monday. By Friday your team finally messaged them. They had already booked a site visit with another installer."
₦2M job — lost to speed
Scenario 2
"You called within the hour. They said they were not ready yet. Your team moved on. Three weeks later that homeowner installed with a competitor who kept following up."
₦5,000 spent — ₦1.8M job lost to silence
Scenario 3
"The lead said 'let me think about it.' Your team had no follow-up sequence, no value-add message, no reason to call back. The deal died without anyone knowing it was still alive."
₦2.4M job — lost to no system

The 3-Month SMS Sequence
W1
Week 1 — Warm Welcome
3 SMS. Positions the installer as a certified expert. Sets expectations. Builds immediate trust before the first call.
3 SMS this week
W2
Weeks 2–4 — Education & Value
3 SMS per week. Power cost comparisons, solar ROI facts, homeowner success stories. Keeps the installer top of mind while building the homeowner's case for solar.
9 SMS across 3 weeks
W5
Weeks 5–8 — Urgency & Social Proof
3 SMS per week. Result stories from other homeowners in the same city. Price increase warnings. Seasonal NEPA pattern data. Moves "thinking about it" to "I need to decide."
12 SMS across 4 weeks
W9
Weeks 9–12 — Final Close Window
3 SMS per week. Direct re-engagement. Limited availability messaging. Last-chance site visit offer. Designed to convert every remaining warm lead before the 90-day window closes.
12 SMS across 4 weeks

4 installer slots currently open across Nigeria
Common Questions

Questions Installers
Always Ask First.

Before you apply, here are the answers to the questions every installer asks before their first batch.

What if a lead does not pick up their phone?

Every lead gives their WhatsApp number voluntarily and is told an installer will contact them. If you call within 1 hour of delivery, contact rates are above 70%. If a lead is completely unreachable after 3 attempts in 24 hours, message us and we review it. Leads that fail our 8-gate check are not delivered and not charged.

How does billing work exactly?

Your ads budget goes directly to faceebook through Tubalcain Ad's account. Each time a qualified lead is delivered to your WhatsApp, ₦5,000 is charged. We invoice you by batch of 10 or 5 based on your selection. You can set a monthly lead cap so your spend stays predictable. No lead, no charge — ever.

Can I pause or stop at any time?

Yes. There is no lock-in, no minimum commitment, and no cancellation fee. If your team is full or you are on a job, message us and we pause your campaign that day. You only pay for leads that have already been delivered.

What cities do you currently cover?

We currently operate in Lagos, Abuja, Port Harcourt, Ibadan, Benin City, Nasarawa, Makurdi, Imo State and other states in Nigeria. Each city has one exclusive slot. Apply and we confirm if your city is still available. New cities open regularly as demand grows.

What is the ₦1.5M+ budget requirement about?

Every homeowner we deliver has confirmed they have the financial capacity to fund a minimum ₦1.5M solar installation. This is verified through their stated monthly power spend (₦30,000+ per month on PHCN and generator combined). We do not deliver anyone who cannot afford your system.

How long before I start receiving leads?

Once you are confirmed and onboarded, your campaign goes live within 48 hours. Most installers receive their first lead within 3–5 days of campaign launch. After that, leads come in based on Facebook ad performance — typically 2–8 per week depending on your city size.

What if the campaign runs for a full week and produces zero qualified leads?

We only charge ₦4,000 per lead delivered, and zero leads delivered means zero charge. Your ad spend went to Facebook for traffic impressions and clicks, that money leaves your hands the moment it hits the ad account, the same as if you ran the ads yourself. What we do when lead volume is low is diagnose the creative and audience targeting, make adjustments, and keep optimising. We have a direct financial incentive to get leads flowing because we only earn when you receive them. We cannot run a charity campaign that produces nothing — it costs us the same management time either way.

Have a question not covered here? Message us directly on WhatsApp — we respond within 2 hours during business hours.
